content.subtitle: Structure
items: if/when/whenever + present tense, present tense

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Established truths, scientific principles, and universal definitions

content.subtitle: Example
items: When sunlight strikes a solar panel, electricity is generated.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + present tense, will + bare infinitive / imperative

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Likely situations in the present or future and their probable outcomes
To provide instructions that depend on a particular condition being met

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If we fail to address rising sea levels, coastal communities will face severe consequences.
If the technician from the IT department calls, let him know I'll be available after lunch.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: A range of modals and semi-modals may replace will in the result clause of first conditionals. These include be going to, can, could, may, might, shall, should, have to and ought to.
examples: We might avert serious damage if we reconsider our energy policies right now.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + past simple / past continuous, would + bare infinitive

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Imaginary or unlikely conditions in the present or future and their hypothetical results

content.subtitle: Example
items: What measures would the city council take if a major flood were to hit the area?

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Other modals may replace would in the result clause of second conditionals. Common alternatives include might and could.
examples: If the traffic weren't so bad, we could stop for coffee on the way.

content.subtitle: Grammar Note
content.description: In British English, both was and were are acceptable after if with first and third person singular. Were tends to appear in more formal writing. American English generally prefers were. In both varieties, were is standard in the expression 'if I were you, ...'
examples: UK: If I was/were a risk-taker, I'd invest everything in that start-up.
US: If I were a risk-taker, I'd invest everything in that start-up.

content.subtitle: Structure
items: if + past perfect, would have + past participle

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Imagined conditions set in the past and their hypothetical results in the past

content.subtitle: Example
items: Far more residents would have been displaced by the landslide if an early warning system hadn't been in place.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
content.description: Other modals may replace would in the result clause of third conditionals. Alternatives include might, could and should.
examples: If you hadn't acted so impulsively, you could have avoided losing the deal entirely.

content.subtitle: Structure and Uses
items: Imagined conditions in the past and their hypothetical results in the present
Imagined conditions in the present and their hypothetical results in the past

content.subtitle: Examples
items: You might not be struggling so much now if you hadn't quit your language lessons early.
If I were a more patient person, I would have listened to her advice at the time.

content.subtitle: First Conditional (formal)
examples: Should the heatwave persist, residents will be urged to conserve water. (= if the heatwave should persist … / If the heatwave persists …)

content.subtitle: Second Conditional (formal)
examples: Were the company to relocate its headquarters, it would still take years for the local economy to adjust. (= If the company were to relocate … / If the company relocated …)

content.subtitle: Third Conditional (formal)
examples: Had Marie Curie not pursued her research so relentlessly, many advances in science might never have occurred. (= If Marie Curie hadn't pursued …)

content.subtitle: Negative Inversion
content.description: When the inverted verb is negative, not is placed after the subject.
examples: Had we not submitted the proposal on time, we would have had no chance of winning the contract.

content.subtitle: Comma Usage
content.description: When the if clause precedes the result clause, a comma is normally used to separate them. When the result clause comes first, no comma is required.
examples: If you cycle to work regularly, you can cut your carbon footprint significantly.
You can cut your carbon footprint significantly if you cycle to work regularly.

content.subtitle: Alternative Condition Words
items: as/so long as
provided/providing (that)
on condition (that)
suppose/supposing
if it wasn't/weren't for + noun
were it not for + noun
but for + noun
if it hadn't been for + noun
had it not been for + noun
if … (should) happen to
if … should
if … happened to
if … were to
if so/not
otherwise
unless
in case of + noun
in case

content.subtitle: Examples
items: We'll head to the coast this weekend as long as the forecast improves.
You may borrow the equipment provided that you return it in perfect condition.
New membership applications are accepted on condition that applicants are at least 16 years old.
Supposing the price of property doubled overnight. How do you think the market would react?
I think I'd find city life very isolating if it wasn't/weren't for my running club.
Were it not for my running club, I think I'd find city life very isolating.
But for your encouragement, I wouldn't have had the confidence to apply for that scholarship.
If it hadn't been for your encouragement, I wouldn't have had the confidence to apply for that scholarship.
Had it not been for your encouragement, I wouldn't have had the confidence to apply for that scholarship.
If you (should) happen to run into Natalie, ask her whether she'd be willing to cover for me next week.
If you should see Natalie, ask her whether she'd be willing to cover for me next week.
If you happened to see a wallet lying on the pavement, what would you do with it?
Do you think air quality would improve if the government were to ban diesel vehicles in city centres?
Are you passionate about sustainability? If so, you might enjoy volunteering with a local environmental group.
You should have your heating system checked before winter, otherwise you could end up wasting a great deal of energy.
Unless consumers change their habits soon, the demand for single-use plastics is going to remain a serious problem.
In case of emergency, proceed to the nearest emergency exit and follow staff instructions.
Put on a waterproof jacket in case the weather turns wet on the way.

content.subtitle: Meanings
items: To express 'if it is true that'
To express 'if you are willing to'
To express 'I'm mentioning this just in case'

content.subtitle: Examples
items: If you were at the seminar, why didn't you bring up the question of data privacy?
If you'll come this way, I'll take you through to the director's office.
If you enjoy hiking, the trails around Lake Bled are absolutely stunning.

sections.title: A: Match to Make Sentences
sections.instructions: Match the beginnings (1-10) with the endings (A-J) to make complete conditional sentences.

items.sentence: If you sign up for the membership today,
If you choose not to respond to the invitation,
If you didn't rush through your meals so quickly,
If there were a couple more volunteers helping,
If you forget to bring your identity card,
If the train isn't running when you get to the station,
If the urban population keeps rising at this pace,
If the traffic wardens didn't issue penalties,
If you ever decided to set up your own company,
If committee members wish to step down,

sections.title: B: Rewrite Using Conditionals
sections.instructions: Rewrite the sentences using an appropriate conditional form, starting with the words given.

items.sentence: Marco didn't bring any snacks, which is why I didn't share mine with him.
items.answer: If Marco had brought snacks, I would have shared mine with him.

items.sentence: They couldn't dispatch the delivery because the warehouse had a system failure.
items.answer: If the warehouse hadn't had a system failure, they would have dispatched the delivery.

items.sentence: Sea temperatures are rising so dramatically that marine biologists are alarmed.
items.answer: If sea temperatures weren't rising so dramatically, marine biologists wouldn't be alarmed.

items.sentence: Did the town flourish because a major railway line passed through it?
items.answer: Would the town have flourished if a major railway line hadn't passed through it?

items.sentence: It's the spectacular waterfall that attracts visitors to that valley.
items.answer: If it weren't for the spectacular waterfall, visitors wouldn't be attracted to that valley.

items.sentence: There was a serious IT outage in the office, so the conference call was cancelled.
items.answer: If there hadn't been a serious IT outage in the office, the conference call wouldn't have been cancelled.

items.sentence: I suggest you get travel insurance, and then you won't be left with unexpected costs.
items.answer: If I were you, I would get travel insurance so that you aren't left with unexpected costs.

items.sentence: The bridge was damaged because the construction company had used substandard materials.
items.answer: If the construction company had used proper materials, the bridge wouldn't have been damaged.

sections.title: C: Complete the Text
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in bold. Add any other words you need.

items.sentence: If I (1) …… (do) that then,
I might never (2) …… (have) so many eye-opening experiences.
If you (3) …… (ask) me now,
to confirm whether any hostels (4) …… (book).
I usually (5) …… (have) enough time to check all the arrangements
especially if any volunteers (6) …… (get) separated from the group
which wouldn't (7) …… (happen)
if the group coordinator (8) …… (be) a little more attentive.
especially if you (9) …… (wander) off the designated route.
That particular incident might (10) …… (be) so dangerous
if a sudden storm (11) …… (start) just as we were heading back.
I shudder to think what might (12) …… (happen) if we had not called for help!

sections.title: D: Circle the Correct Word or Phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: If we hadn't missed that connection back at the airport, we wouldn't be / have been stranded here now.
Should / Would you require any additional information about the course, please get in touch with the admissions team.
Had they not / Hadn't they spotted the gas leak early, a serious explosion could have occurred.
If you submitted / had submitted your application when the window first opened, you wouldn't be on the waiting list now.
Were all the delegates to join the video call, would the platform have / have had enough capacity for everyone?
Had the authorities responded more promptly, residents wouldn't be worrying / have worried so much about air quality.
Cate Blanchett wouldn't be the icon she is today had she not / should she not have taken on such challenging roles early in her career.
Weren't we / Were we not to introduce tighter emissions controls, pollution levels would become critical within a decade.
It would be nearly impossible to make the last train even if you left / had left the office two hours earlier today.
You can reach our technical team on extension 237 should / could you run into any difficulties.
Shouldn't you / Should you not receive a tracking update within 48 hours, please contact our customer service team.
I would have asked the security guard to check his ID before letting him through if I were / had been you.

sections.title: E: Complete with the Words in the Box
sections.instructions: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: Are you looking for a career change, and, if …… , do you have any idea what direction you'd like to take?
I'm perfectly happy to lend you my notes, as …… as you return them before the exam.
The outdoor concert should go ahead as planned, …… we don't face the same issues with the sound system as last time.
It would be great to meet up on Thursday, …… you've already got something else on.
Would you be able to stay at the office a while longer? I need to be on hand in …… the clients arrive early.
Your strategy sounds promising in theory, but just …… the market shifts unexpectedly. What's your contingency plan?
Always save your work every few minutes, …… you risk losing everything if the system crashes.
The athlete was permitted to return to training on …… she underwent weekly medical assessments.

sections.title: F: Write One Word in Each Gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I would never have managed to complete the renovation if …… wasn't for all the support from my neighbours.
If it hadn't …… for the fact that her mentor had connections in the industry, Clara would never have landed that role.
How would you feel if someone happened …… reverse into your brand-new car in a car park?
…… for the quick instincts of a nearby paramedic, the situation could have turned fatal.
I wouldn't be the slightest bit surprised …… Marcus were to announce tomorrow that he was leaving the country.
Don't forget to pass on the revised agenda to Leila if you …… happen to see her at the seminar.
International cycling tours would be far more appealing to families …… it not for the hazards of riding on busy roads.
If it hadn't been …… her coach's unwavering belief in her, I doubt Sofia would have made it to the finals.
I would have lost the contract …… it not been for a last-minute revision of the proposal.
If you …… come across any problems during the installation process, our helpline is open around the clock.

sections.title: G: Rewrite the Sentences
sections.instructions: Rewrite the sentences, starting with the words given.

items.sentence: The sole reason the team made it to the final was Carla's extraordinary goal in extra time.
items.answer: We wouldn't have made it to the final if it hadn't been for Carla's extraordinary goal in extra time.

items.sentence: The rapid deployment of emergency services prevented the chemical spill from reaching the river.
items.answer: If it hadn't been for the rapid deployment of emergency services, the chemical spill would have reached the river.

items.sentence: None of the cyclists was hurt because of the marshal's prompt action in stopping the traffic.
items.answer: But for the marshal's prompt action in stopping the traffic, some of the cyclists would have been hurt.

items.sentence: We'll have to reschedule the interview if Daniel doesn't confirm his availability by tomorrow.
items.answer: Unless Daniel confirms his availability by tomorrow, we'll have to reschedule the interview.

items.sentence: Professor Huang would be quite lonely if she didn't have her regular video calls with former students.
items.answer: Were it not for her regular video calls with former students, Professor Huang would be quite lonely.

items.sentence: Here's my business card, because you might want to contact me later.
items.answer: Here's my business card in case you want to contact me later.

items.sentence: Hand in the report by Thursday and you can leave early on Friday.
items.answer: Providing you hand in the report by Thursday, you can leave early on Friday.

items.sentence: It was Inspector García who prevented the suspects from slipping away.
items.answer: Had it not been for Inspector García, the suspects would have slipped away.

sections.title: H: Choose the Correct Answer
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer A or B.

items.sentence: If you witnessed a hit-and-run accident near your home, …… the police?
Make sure you confirm your reservation at least 24 hours in advance, …… they may give your table to someone else.
Were …… my grandfather, I would never have emigrated from the island in the first place.
If I …… bump into Sophie at the conference, I'll pass on your message.
The series would have been perfect …… the disappointing finale.
If you hadn't ignored those early warning signs, you …… in this predicament now.
Do you think that if we …… a different supplier, we might not have had all these quality issues?
Unless the package …… by Friday morning, I'm going to contact the delivery company.
What if I …… you that there's every chance I can arrange a private tour for your group?
If there …… happen to be any discrepancy in your invoice, please notify our accounts department immediately.

sections.title: I: Renewable Energy Cloze Text
sections.instructions: Complete the text with the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Had (1) …… not been for the pioneering research into solar technology in the 1970s,
our current energy landscape would look very different. In fact, (2) …… this research to be carried out today,
scientists (3) …… undoubtedly employ far more advanced methods.
Back then, there (4) …… considerable scepticism about whether solar panels could ever be cost-effective.
If you (5) …… able to visit one of today's large-scale solar farms,
you (6) …… be amazed by the sheer scale of the installations.
Prolonged exposure to ultraviolet radiation could cause lasting harm (7) …… workers were to go without adequate protection,
and certain chemicals used in battery production can damage your health (8) …… you exposed to them regularly.
We might (9) …… have made such rapid progress
(10) …… it not been for the advocacy of several key environmental organisations,
and public investment might have dried up (11) …… the scientific consensus not been so overwhelming.
but (12) …… which the renewable energy revolution of today would have been impossible.

sections.title: J: Keyword Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence: Without the project manager's expertise, the launch would never have gone so smoothly.
items.answer: Had it not been for the project manager's expertise, the launch would never have gone so smoothly.

items.sentence: Unless there are any last-minute changes, we'll pick you up at eight o'clock sharp.
items.answer: Provided there are no last-minute changes, we'll pick you up at eight o'clock sharp.

items.sentence: If you registered for the workshop now, there's a strong chance you would secure a place.
items.answer: Were you to register for the workshop now, there's a strong chance you would secure a place.

items.sentence: I had no idea about the revised timetable, so I didn't arrange alternative transport.
items.answer: If I'd known about the revised timetable, I would have arranged alternative transport.

items.sentence: It might get very busy later, so keep a table reserved.
items.answer: Keep a table reserved in case it gets very busy later.

items.sentence: I'd love to spend a year travelling if I didn't have so many work commitments.
items.answer: Were it not for my work commitments, I'd love to spend a year travelling.

items.sentence: Twenty years ago, critics would have dismissed this kind of performance entirely.
items.answer: Had critics seen this kind of performance twenty years ago, they would have dismissed it entirely.

items.sentence: Students are permitted to use the reference library if they present a valid student card.
items.answer: Students are permitted to use the reference library on condition that they present a valid student card.
